title.en: Defective Product? Your Rights to Replacement, Refund and Compensation

answerBox.en: A defective product entitles you to repair, replacement or refund — your election strengthens as the defect proves persistent. The Consumer Protection Act, 2019 added product liability: manufacturers and sellers answer for harm caused by defective goods. Complain in writing within the warranty, escalate via 1915, and file on e-Daakhil within 2 years; commissions can order testing of the product.

en.title: Document the defect trail
en.summary: Invoice, warranty, job sheets — every service visit on paper.
en.detail: Keep the invoice and warranty card, and for every service interaction insist on a **job sheet** recording the complaint and what was done. Photograph/video the defect. The pattern — **repeated repairs for the same defect** — is what converts a "repair" case into a **replacement/refund** case; commissions routinely order replacement where a product fails multiple times within warranty (the "inherent manufacturing defect" inference).

en.title: Escalate: brand heads, then 1915
en.summary: Skip the call-centre loop; write to nodal/appellate contacts.
en.detail: Email the manufacturer's **nodal/grievance contact** (published on brand sites) with the trail and a clear election — replace or refund — and a 15-day deadline. Register on **[consumerhelpline.gov.in / 1915](https://consumerhelpline.gov.in)**: brands respond to NCH dockets far faster than to tickets. Under the **2019 Act's product-liability chapter**, manufacturer AND seller liability covers manufacturing defects, design defects, warranty deviations, and inadequate instructions/warnings.

en.title: Legal notice, then e-Daakhil with testing
en.summary: Commissions can send the product for lab analysis.
en.detail: A **₹999 advocate's notice** electing replacement/refund resolves many matters. Failing that, file on **[e-Daakhil](https://edaakhil.nic.in)** (see our [consumer forum guide](/help/consumer-complaints/consumer-forum-complaint-guide)) — where the defect is technical and disputed, **Section 38(2)(c)** lets the commission send the product to an appropriate **laboratory for testing** (small deposit, added to your claim). Claim: replacement or refund with interest, compensation for loss/harassment, and litigation costs.

en.title: Know the seller's standard deflections
en.summary: "Physical damage", "warranty void", "only repair" — and the answers.
en.detail: Deflections and answers: **"physical damage — warranty void"** (they must prove the damage caused the defect; job sheets showing the defect predates any damage defeat this); **"warranty only covers repair"** — warranty terms cannot defeat statutory rights where the product has an inherent defect; **"third-party service voided it"** — post-2019 unfair-contract scrutiny cuts against blanket voiding. Refuse to hand over the *original* invoice/product without receipts, and never accept a refurbished unit as "replacement" unless you agree in writing.

rows.label: e-Daakhil + lab testing
rows.note: Testing costs claimable in the award

en.footnote: For vehicles and high-value appliances with repeated failures, replacement-or-refund orders are common — persistence plus paper beats the service-loop attrition strategy every time.

en: kharab product replace nahi kar rahe

en.q: Can I demand a refund instead of endless repairs?
en.a: Yes, once the pattern shows an inherent defect — multiple failed repairs for the same fault within warranty. Commissions order replacement or refund with interest in exactly these cases; your job-sheet trail is the proof. Elect your remedy explicitly in the notice.

en.q: The defect appeared just after warranty expired. Any rights?
en.a: Possibly — where the defect is latent/manufacturing in origin, courts have granted relief beyond warranty, especially for short-warranty durable goods, and the 2019 product-liability chapter is not warranty-bound. It is harder but not hopeless; the 2-year limitation runs from the defect surfacing.

en.q: Who do I sue — the seller, the brand, or both?
en.a: Both (and the e-commerce platform if bought online). The 2019 Act's product-liability provisions reach manufacturers, product sellers and service providers; joint arraying prevents finger-pointing and maximises recovery.

en.q: What does product testing before the commission involve?
en.a: Where the defect is disputed, the commission sends the product to an appropriate laboratory (Section 38(2)(c)) with a modest deposit from you; the report becomes the technical backbone of the case, and testing costs are added to compensation when you win.

en.q: The service centre kept my product and returns nothing. What now?
en.a: Demand its return/replacement in writing with the job sheet number; retention beyond reasonable repair time is itself deficiency. Include it in the notice and complaint — commissions award the product's value plus compensation for the retention.

reviewer: Adv. Sneha Iyer, Bar Council of Tamil Nadu & Puducherry, TN/1187/2013
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
